announcements.headline: Victorian Mobilisation at Walhalla Project
summary: Infinity Mining has mobilised field crews to the Walhalla Gold Project in Victoria, signaling active exploration progress and commitment to advancing this historically significant gold district.

On-ground mobilisation represents a tangible deployment of capital toward exploration activities, which for a junior miner is a key catalyst for newsflow and potential resource delineation that could re-rate the stock.

Investors should watch for upcoming drill results or geochemical sampling data from Walhalla as the next material catalyst; the market-sensitive designation suggests management views this mobilisation as a meaningful inflection point in the project timeline.

announcements.headline: UK Europe Investor Presentation
summary: St Barbara is actively marketing to UK/European institutional investors, signaling efforts to broaden its shareholder register and potentially attract new capital inflows ahead of key project milestones.

The market-sensitive designation suggests the presentation likely contains material updates on operations, financials, or strategic direction beyond routine corporate messaging — potentially including updated guidance or project economics.

Expanded investor engagement in international markets may be a precursor to future capital raising activity or strategic transactions, with management seeking to build institutional support across multiple jurisdictions.

announcements.headline: 2026 Challenger Investor Day
summary: Challenger Limited is hosting a 2026 Investor Day, signaling management's intent to communicate strategic direction, growth initiatives, and potentially updated financial targets to the institutional investor community.

The announcement is flagged as market sensitive, suggesting the Investor Day will likely include material disclosures — potentially revised guidance, capital allocation plans, or new product/distribution strategies within its Life and Funds Management divisions.

Investor Days for annuity and funds management businesses like Challenger often serve as catalysts when management articulates medium-term earnings growth pathways; investors should watch for updated normalised profit guidance, book growth targets, and margin outlook.

announcements.headline: High-Grade Fluorspar Identified at the Mallard Prospect
summary: Mont Royal Resources has identified high-grade fluorspar (calcium fluoride) mineralisation at the Mallard Prospect, a critical mineral used in aluminium smelting, steelmaking, and fluorochemicals — positioning the company within a supply-constrained market dominated by China and Mexico.

High-grade fluorspar discoveries are relatively rare outside established mining jurisdictions, and if confirmed through systematic drilling, this could represent a meaningful de-risking event for the project and unlock significant value given fluorspar's designation as a critical mineral by multiple governments.

Next steps will likely involve follow-up sampling, resource definition drilling, and metallurgical testwork at Mallard; investors should watch for assay results and grade continuity data to assess whether the prospect can support an economically viable deposit.

announcements.headline: New data confirms cancer-fighting potential
summary: Noxopharm has released new preclinical or clinical data reinforcing the cancer-fighting mechanism of its lead drug candidates, a key catalyst for a micro-cap biotech where pipeline validation directly drives re-rating potential.

As a pre-revenue biotech, the significance of this data lies in its ability to de-risk future clinical milestones and potentially attract partnership interest or licensing deals, which are critical given the company's limited cash runway and ongoing need for capital.

Investors should watch for follow-up announcements detailing trial design, regulatory pathway clarity, and any partnership discussions — these will determine whether the data translates into meaningful value inflection or remains incremental.

announcements.headline: High Grade Results at Clement Prospect up to 26.53g/t Gold
summary: Nexus Minerals reports exceptional high-grade gold intercepts at the Clement Prospect, with results up to 26.53g/t gold — significantly above typical exploration-stage grades and potentially indicative of a high-quality orebody that could materially de-risk the project.

High-grade results of this calibre at Clement are likely to attract market attention and could catalyse a re-rating of the stock, particularly if mineralisation demonstrates continuity and sufficient width to support economic extraction in future resource estimates.

Investors should watch for follow-up drilling programs to test strike and depth extensions at Clement, as well as any updated mineral resource estimates — the pathway from high-grade discovery hits to a defined resource will be the key value inflection point for the company.

announcements.headline: IP Survey Expanded to Test Paradise City, Hurley & T17
summary: Terrain Minerals is expanding its Induced Polarisation (IP) survey program to cover three additional targets — Paradise City, Hurley, and T17 — signalling growing confidence in the prospectivity of its exploration portfolio and a systematic approach to target generation.

The decision to extend the geophysical survey scope suggests initial IP results from earlier lines were sufficiently encouraging to justify additional expenditure, a positive technical signal for investors tracking drill-target prioritisation at this pre-resource stage.

Successful IP anomaly delineation across multiple prospects could accelerate the pathway to drill planning and resource definition, though investors should monitor the company's cash position given the incremental survey costs and the absence of near-term revenue generation.

announcements.headline: Thunderbird Operations Update
summary: Sheffield Resources has issued a market-sensitive operational update on its flagship Thunderbird mineral sands project, signaling material developments in production ramp-up or operational performance that could impact near-term revenue trajectory.

Thunderbird is one of Australia's largest undeveloped mineral sands deposits; any update on production rates, concentrate quality, or cost metrics will be closely scrutinized by investors given the project's significance to Sheffield's valuation and cash flow generation.

Investors should monitor whether the update addresses commissioning milestones, throughput targets, or any operational challenges that could affect the timeline to steady-state production and the company's cash runway ahead of sustained revenue generation.

announcements.headline: Change in substantial holding
summary: A substantial holder in Unith Ltd has altered their position, triggering a mandatory disclosure under ASX substantial holding rules — investors should monitor whether this reflects accumulation or distribution by a key shareholder.

The notice is flagged as not market sensitive, suggesting the change in holding is relatively modest or procedural in nature, likely crossing or adjusting around the 5% threshold without signaling a dramatic shift in ownership dynamics.

Institutional investors should track whether this forms part of a broader pattern of insider or institutional repositioning in Unith Ltd, particularly given the company's micro-cap status where even small changes in substantial holdings can meaningfully affect liquidity and free float.

announcements.headline: SPH - Macquarie - ceasing to hold substantial holding
summary: Macquarie Group has ceased to hold a substantial holding in Contact Energy Limited, indicating its stake has fallen below the 5% disclosure threshold, signaling a meaningful reduction in institutional ownership.

The exit of a major institutional holder like Macquarie may reflect portfolio rebalancing or a shift in conviction on the NZ/Australian energy sector, potentially increasing available free float and short-term selling pressure.
Investors should monitor whether other substantial holders adjust positions in response, and whether this creates an opportunity for new institutional accumulation at potentially lower price levels.

announcements.company: NEW ZEALAND KING SALMON INVESTMENTS LIMITED
announcements.headline: NZK - Investor Presentation
summary: New Zealand King Salmon has released a market-sensitive investor presentation, likely outlining strategic updates or operational progress that the company considers material to its valuation and investor decision-making.

As a dual-listed aquaculture company, any presentation flagged as market sensitive likely contains forward-looking guidance, capital structure updates, or significant operational milestones related to its open ocean farming transition strategy.

Investors should scrutinize the presentation for details on cash runway sustainability, progress on blue water farming consents, and any revised production or revenue forecasts that could reshape the investment thesis.

announcements.company: NEW ZEALAND KING SALMON INVESTMENTS LIMITED
announcements.headline: NZK - 1HY26 Media Announcement
summary: New Zealand King Salmon Investments releases 1HY26 results update, signaling a key reporting milestone for the aquaculture company listed on both NZX and ASX.

As a media announcement accompanying half-year results, this likely contains headline financial metrics including revenue, EBITDA, and net profit that will be closely scrutinized given the company's ongoing operational transformation and shift toward open ocean farming.

Investors should watch for commentary on harvest volumes, pricing trends for premium king salmon, balance sheet health, and progress on the company's strategic pivot to more sustainable farming locations — all critical to the turnaround thesis.

announcements.company: NEW ZEALAND KING SALMON INVESTMENTS LIMITED
announcements.headline: NZK - Results Announcement
summary: New Zealand King Salmon Investments has released its results announcement, a market-sensitive disclosure that will detail the company's financial performance and operational metrics for the reporting period.

As a salmon aquaculture company listed on the ASX, key metrics to watch include revenue trends, harvest volumes, feed conversion ratios, and any impairments or write-downs related to fish mortality or environmental challenges that have historically pressured margins.

Investors should focus on the company's cash position, debt covenants, and forward guidance on production volumes, particularly given NZK's ongoing strategic transformation toward open ocean farming and its capital-intensive growth plans.

announcements.headline: FY26 results - presentation
summary: Ryman Healthcare's FY26 results presentation is market-sensitive, signaling material financial updates that institutional investors should assess for earnings trajectory, occupancy trends, and development pipeline progress across its NZ/AU retirement village portfolio.

Key metrics to watch include underlying profit, net debt levels, cash flow from resales and new sales settlements, and any changes to gearing covenants — all critical for evaluating the company's balance sheet resilience amid elevated construction costs and interest rates.

Forward guidance on village development pipeline, capital management strategy, and any revision to medium-term delivery targets will be pivotal in determining whether Ryman can sustain its growth ambitions without further equity dilution.

announcements.headline: FY26 results - media release
summary: Ryman Healthcare's FY26 results release as a market-sensitive announcement signals material financial updates for the integrated retirement living and aged care operator, likely covering occupancy rates, development pipeline progress, and underlying profit metrics across NZ and Australian operations.

Key metrics to watch include net operating cashflow trajectory, resale gains, new sales volumes, and any movement in gearing ratios — critical for assessing whether Ryman's balance sheet deleveraging strategy is on track following prior periods of elevated debt concerns.

Forward guidance on development completions, capital expenditure discipline, and potential dividend reinstatement will be pivotal for institutional positioning, particularly given the sector's sensitivity to interest rates and construction cost pressures in both NZ and Australian markets.

announcements.headline: FY26 result - NZX announcement numbers
summary: Ryman Healthcare has released NZX announcement numbers related to its FY26 result, signaling an administrative/procedural update rather than substantive financial disclosure at this stage.

As a dual-listed aged care and retirement village operator, any FY26 result detail will be closely watched for occupancy trends, development pipeline progress, and debt covenant compliance given the sector's capital-intensive nature.
Investors should await the full FY26 results release for material financial metrics including underlying profit, cash flow from operations, and net debt position to assess valuation implications.

announcements.headline: Infratil Full Year Results for the year ended 31 March 2026
summary: Infratil reports full-year results for FY26 (year ended 31 March 2026), a market-sensitive release that will be closely scrutinized for portfolio company performance across data centers (CDC), renewable energy (Longroad, Galileo), and healthcare (Pacific Radiology) segments.

As a diversified infrastructure investor with significant exposure to high-growth digital infrastructure and energy transition themes, the results will reveal whether Infratil's NAV growth trajectory and dividend capacity remain intact amid evolving capital deployment across its portfolio.

Investors will focus on updated valuations of key assets (particularly CDC Data Centres given AI-driven demand), capital management signals including any revised dividend guidance, and forward-looking commentary on the pipeline for new investments and potential asset recycling.

announcements.headline: Preliminary Final Report
summary: Fisher & Paykel Healthcare's Preliminary Final Report signals full-year results disclosure, a key catalyst for re-rating given the company's dual ASX/NZX listing and significant institutional following in respiratory and acute care devices.

Market-sensitive designation indicates material financial metrics are being reported — investors should focus on revenue trajectory in Hospital and Homecare product groups, operating margin trends, and any FX impacts from NZD/USD movements on earnings.

As a preliminary report, this precedes the full annual report and sets the tone for FY guidance and capital allocation decisions including dividends, R&D investment, and capacity expansion plans — critical for forward earnings models.
